title: PHP Process Fork Handling Flow
interaction_flow: PHPUserCode → ddtrace_pcntl_fork: pcntl_fork() [intercepted by ddtrace handler]
ddtrace_sidecar_handle_fork → Sidecar: reconnect (worker→parent listener, or start new master)
dd_internal_handle_fork → RemoteConfig: ddog_shutdown_remote_config() [reset remote config state]
dd_internal_handle_fork → PRNG: ddtrace_seed_prng() [reseed random for child]
dd_internal_handle_fork → RuntimeID: ddtrace_generate_runtime_id() [new runtime ID for child]
dd_internal_handle_fork → SpanStack: ddtrace_free_span_stacks(true) [discard parent spans in child]
dd_internal_handle_fork → SpanStack: ddtrace_init_span_stacks() [fresh span stack for child]
dd_internal_handle_fork → COMSWriter: ddtrace_coms_init_and_start_writer() [start fresh writer in child, !SIDECAR, !WIN32]
dd_handle_fork → [PARENT branch if pid>0]: ddtrace_coms_restart_writer() [only if JOIN_BGS_BEFORE_FORK && !SIDECAR]
key_relationships: ddtrace_pcntl_fork wraps OS pcntl_fork (also ddtrace_pcntl_rfork PHP>=8.1, ddtrace_pcntl_forkx PHP>=8.2)
dd_handle_fork dispatches to dd_internal_handle_fork (child) or ddtrace_coms_restart_writer (parent)
dd_internal_handle_fork orchestrates: COMSWriter cleanup, SidecarReconnect, RemoteConfigReset, PRNG reseed, SpanStack reset
DDTrace\Internal::handle_fork() is the PHP-level API that calls dd_internal_handle_fork() directly
dd_trace_disable_in_request() calls ddtrace_disable_tracing_in_current_request() — same primitive used in fork child path
dd_trace_reset() is a stub (TODO) that returns bool, not used in fork path
dd_trace_coms_trigger_writer_flush() calls ddtrace_coms_trigger_writer_flush() — triggers async flush of buffered spans via COMSWriter
Tracer::flush() (DDTrace namespace) closes all spans and force-sends to agent — higher level than coms trigger
COMSWriter subsystem (coms.c/coms.h): manages background sender thread and curl transport
Sidecar mode (DD_TRACE_SIDECAR_TRACE_SENDER) bypasses COMSWriter — uses Rust FFI sidecar process instead
branching_conditions: fork return value == 0 → child process path (dd_internal_handle_fork)
fork return value > 0 → parent process path (restart writer if JOIN_BGS_BEFORE_FORK)
JOIN_BGS_BEFORE_FORK defined only under ASAN builds — controls pre/post-fork writer join
DD_TRACE_SIDECAR_TRACE_SENDER=true → skip COMSWriter curl operations, use sidecar transport
DD_TRACE_FORKED_PROCESS=true → keep tracing enabled in child; false (default) → disable tracing in child
DD_TRACE_GENERATE_ROOT_SPAN=true → push new root span in child after stack reset
DD_DISTRIBUTED_TRACING=true → capture parent trace/span IDs as distributed context in child
php_api_signatures: DDTrace\Internal\handle_fork(): void  [internal, called post-fork in child]
dd_trace_disable_in_request(): bool  [disables tracing for current request]
dd_trace_reset(): bool  [stub/noop, returns true if not globally disabled]
dd_trace_coms_trigger_writer_flush(): int  [triggers async writer flush, returns count]
DDTrace\Tracer::flush(): void  [closes all spans + force-sends to agent]
dd_trace_synchronous_flush(int $timeout=100): void  [blocking flush with timeout ms]
important_notes: Fork handling is transparent to PHP code: ddtrace intercepts pcntl_fork/rfork/forkx at MINIT via datadog_php_install_handler
Child process must NOT inherit parent's COMSWriter background thread (POSIX: only calling thread is duplicated) — hence cleanup + reinit
Sidecar transport (ddog/Rust FFI) maintains its own connection state, requires ddtrace_sidecar_handle_fork() reconnect logic
Default behavior: tracing is DISABLED in forked child unless DD_TRACE_FORKED_PROCESS=true
Parent span IDs are preserved as distributed context if DD_DISTRIBUTED_TRACING=true, enabling parent→child trace linking
dd_trace_coms_trigger_writer_flush is the parent-side mechanism to flush buffered spans before child takes over
Remote config and agent info readers are dropped and re-created in child to avoid shared-memory conflicts
